lastReleaseMessage: map beeswax to the honey illustration

barRaise.topFinding: Best run in a week: the v0.17.0 batch closed eight of yesterday's nine MEDIUMs in one commit (spec drift, the 47-em-dash sweep, the three-way name split, the .bak clutter, the saved-state schema version, the html2canvas doc gap, and the dead category wiring), and the 06-12 canonical audit confirms spec and code in near-total alignment at v0.18.1. Verdict stays needs-attention only because the apply-but-do-not-commit pattern recurred: nine files of real fixes (a localStorage quota-error feature, the release.ps1 clean-tree guard, the README refresh) sit uncommitted, so workTreeClean is wrong again and none of it is live. The four residual MEDIUMs are the unshipped tree, the standing 1020MB .git history bloat that needs a decide-and-close, the drifted dashboard count fields, and YaA queue items now carrying inconsistent schemas. The label engine is healthy for the fifth run; one commit and release closes most of it.
